summary.intent: Enable the standalone Tekton Dashboard on the artc2023 OpenShift cluster and connect it to the existing Tekton Results infrastructure for log retention and viewing pipeline runs.
summary.outcome: Cleaned up a broken 2-year-old manual installation, deployed Tekton Dashboard v0.66.0 with external logs integration to Tekton Results API, switched it to read-write mode, and configured namespace-scoped RBAC for write access to art-cd namespace only.

repo: OpenShift Pipelines operator v1.21.0 does not include a TektonDashboard controller or CRD - the dashboard must be installed manually from upstream
The cluster uses `openshift-pipelines` namespace instead of upstream's `tekton-pipelines` default
Console-plugin (pipelines-console-plugin) provides limited UI in OpenShift Console but is separate from standalone Tekton Dashboard
Log retrieval flow: Dashboard → Results API (v1alpha3) → PostgreSQL → Loki Gateway → S3 bucket (artc2023-artcd-logs)
Tekton Dashboard's --external-logs expects /<namespace>/<pod>/<container> URL pattern, which Results API v1alpha3 supports at /apis/results.tekton.dev/v1alpha3/parents
workflow: Tekton Dashboard no longer ships OpenShift-specific release manifests (openshift-tekton-dashboard-release.yaml removed) - must adapt standard release.yaml
OpenShift requires creating a Route with edge TLS termination rather than using Ingress
Upstream Tekton Dashboard manifest defaults to --read-only=true and namespace tekton-pipelines, both need changing for OpenShift
RBAC should be namespace-scoped (Role/RoleBinding) for write access rather than cluster-wide ClusterRole modifications
Dashboard ServiceAccount needs create/delete/update/patch verbs on tekton.dev resources (pipelineruns, taskruns, etc.) for rerun functionality

friction: Old manually-installed dashboard deployment had been stuck in ImagePullBackOff for ~2 years pulling from inaccessible gcr.io registry
Initial confusion about whether OpenShift Pipelines operator manages the dashboard (it doesn't)
OpenShift-specific release manifest URL pattern no longer exists in newer Tekton releases
Switching to read-write mode caused 403 errors until RBAC was updated with write permissions
Had to manually adapt namespace references from tekton-pipelines to openshift-pipelines throughout the manifest
open_items: TektonConfig remains in 'Not Ready' state due to TektonAddon reconciliation issue (addon-custom-pipelinestemplate failing webhook validation)
Could extend dashboard write access to additional namespaces beyond art-cd as needed
No end-to-end verification of log retrieval from S3 through Results API integration was performed
PipelinesAsCode tekton-dashboard-url was updated but integration not tested
